evals.expected_output: tmp/ref/capture/ directory with full-page screenshots in static/ref/ and scroll video in scroll-video/ref/, validated as non-blank and non-bot-detection.
expectations: Creates tmp/ref/capture/ directory structure
Opens site with agent-browser
Sets viewport to 1440x900
Takes full-page screenshot(s) to static/ref/
Records full scroll video to scroll-video/ref/
Validates screenshots are not blank or bot-detection pages

evals.prompt: capture https://playbook.ebay.com — detect all scroll-triggered animations
evals.expected_output: regions.json with scroll array listing detected elements, their from/to scrollY ranges, and changedProperties. Videos recorded for each scroll region.
expectations: Scrolls through page measuring computed style changes
Identifies elements where transform/opacity/clipPath changes on scroll
Records scroll regions with from/to scrollY ranges in regions.json
Captures video for each detected scroll transition region
Filters out duplicates and elements < 50px

evals.prompt: capture hover effects from a site with interactive buttons and cards
evals.expected_output: regions.json with hover array listing verified hover elements. Videos recorded in in/hold/out sequence for each element. Total regions ≤ 20.
expectations: Detects elements with transitionDuration !== '0s'
Verifies hover actually changes computed styles before including
Groups sibling elements with same transition under parent
Records hover in/hold/out video for each verified element
Keeps total regions ≤ 20

evals.prompt: capture cursor-reactive parallax effect with 10x10 matrix
evals.expected_output: transitions/ref/mousemove-<name>.mp4 — single raster-path video sweeping the 10x10 grid. regions.json has mousemove entry with triggerType 'mousemove'.
expectations: Detects mousemove listeners or cursor-reactive class patterns
Tags region with triggerType 'mousemove' in regions.json
Captures single raster-path video sweeping all 100 grid points
Saves video to transitions/ref/mousemove-<name>.mp4
No separate matrix PNG images needed — video is the only artifact

evals.prompt: capture carousel auto-rotation from a site with a slideshow
evals.expected_output: regions.json with timer array including interval_ms estimate. Video recorded covering 2-3 full cycles.
expectations: Detects carousel/slider/swiper elements
Records video waiting for 2-3 full cycles
Saves interval_ms estimate in regions.json

evals.prompt: compare https://example.com vs http://localhost:3000 — generate comparison page
evals.expected_output: compare.html with side-by-side paired video sync, raster-path videos for cursor-reactive elements, and Play All button. URL presented to user.
expectations: Captures impl with identical parameters as ref
Generates compare.html with side-by-side layout
Includes paired video sync (play one, other follows)
Includes paired raster-path videos for cursor-reactive elements (no PNG matrix grids)
Includes Play All button
Serves comparison page and presents URL to user

evals.expected_output: Bot detection detected on first screenshot, retried up to 2 times with increased wait, then reported to user with options: retry, manual capture, or provide screenshots.
expectations: Detects bot-detection page on first screenshot (Cloudflare challenge)
Retries up to 2 times with increased wait
On 3rd failure, reports to user with options: retry, manual capture, or provide screenshots
Does not crash or hang indefinitely

evals.prompt: capture a SPA where content only appears after scrolling
evals.expected_output: All lazy-loaded sections captured accurately. Each scroll position waited for content to settle (800ms+) before screenshot.
expectations: Scrolls to each position before capturing
Waits for content to appear (800ms+ settle time)
Retries with longer wait if screenshot shows empty content
Accurately captures all lazy-loaded sections

evals.prompt: capture all transitions and save to regions.json
evals.expected_output: regions.json with url, capturedAt, page dimensions, and fully populated scroll/hover/mousemove/timer arrays. Each hover region includes triggerType field.
expectations: regions.json has url, capturedAt, page dimensions
scroll array has name, from, to, selector, changedProperties, triggerType ('scroll-driven')
hover array has name, selector, y, bounds, transitionDuration, triggerType
triggerType values are one of: css-hover, js-class, intersection, scroll-driven, mousemove, auto-timer
js-class regions include triggerClass field
mousemove array has name, selector, y, bounds, triggerType ('mousemove')
timer array has name, selector, y, bounds, triggerType ('auto-timer'), interval_ms

evals.prompt: capture a site with 50+ nav links that all have the same hover transition
evals.expected_output: regions.json with deduplicated hover regions — siblings grouped under parent, total ≤ 20. No redundant individual-link entries.
expectations: Groups sibling elements under parent instead of listing all 50
Applies size threshold (> 50px)
Verifies actual style change before including
Total regions ≤ 20
Prioritizes unique effects over repeated ones

evals.prompt: capture a scroll transition for an element located 5000px down the page
evals.expected_output: Scroll video correctly shows the element at the target scroll position (not stuck at y=0). record start followed by scroll eval and viewport reset.
expectations: Issues scroll eval AFTER record start (not before)
Calls set viewport 1440 900 after record start
Waits 3000ms for page to reload in recording context
Scrolls to target position with scrollIntoView or scrollTo eval
Takes verify screenshot before recording to confirm correct position
Crops blank start frames using stdev threshold script

evals.prompt: capture a card that uses a JS class toggle for its hover effect
evals.expected_output: Hover video shows the card animation triggered by toggling the correct CSS class via eval, not by a simple hover command. regions.json has triggerType 'js-class' with triggerClass field.
expectations: Detects that the effect is JS-driven (class toggle), not CSS :hover
Tags region with triggerType 'js-class' and triggerClass in regions.json
Activates effect by adding the class via eval (not agent-browser hover)
Records in/hold/out sequence using class add/remove
Video shows actual animation, not a blank or wrong-state recording

evals.prompt: capture a section that fades in when scrolled into view (IntersectionObserver-triggered animation)
evals.expected_output: Video shows the element animating as it enters the viewport. regions.json has triggerType 'intersection'. Activation uses smooth scroll into view, not agent-browser hover.
expectations: Detects intersection trigger via data-in-view attribute or in-view/inview class pattern
Tags region with triggerType 'intersection' in regions.json
Resets in-view state before recording (removes in-view classes)
Activates by smooth-scrolling element into viewport (not hover command)
Video shows entry animation playing as element scrolls into view

evals.prompt: generate comparison page where two videos have different durations
evals.expected_output: compare.html where both videos play fully to their own end. Shorter video finishing does not pause the longer one. pause events from buffering do not halt playback.
expectations: Video sync uses busy flag to prevent recursive play loops
pause listener checks !a.ended before pausing paired video
ended event does NOT pause the paired video
seeked events sync currentTime between paired videos
Both videos play to their individual ends independently
